title: Tokio: An Asynchronous Rust Runtime Platform
summary: Tokio is an event-driven, non-blocking I/O platform for writing asynchronous applications with the Rust programming language. It provides a multi-threaded, work-stealing task scheduler.
seller_pitch: Build reliable network applications without sacrificing speed. Tokio is designed to support millions of concurrent connections out-of-the-box.
why_it_matters: Standardizes async networking in Rust, paving the way for hyper-performant web servers and database drivers.
build_opportunities: Develop real-time WebSocket messaging systems in Rust using Tokio and Axum
Build high-throughput reverse proxies with custom routing filters
risks: Steep learning curve of Rust lifetime management and pin projection in async contexts
Debugging difficulty during deep work-stealing thread migrations

verification_status.notes: Highly validated, battle-tested asynchronous execution architecture. Fundamental foundation of the modern Rust ecosystem.
